      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +

                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1154:{"acceptance":"- [ ] ddx bead review command produces a well-formed review prompt
- [ ] Template documented (required sections, expected output contract)
- [ ] Reads governing-doc refs from bead fields
- [ ] Used by the post-review path from gap #1","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T05:04:15Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"5aa2161abe858a16200bcc13a2e89ce38627bc35","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.671218963Z","description":"Manual review prompt construction is tedious and inconsistent. A command that reads a bead and produces a review-ready prompt (AC + commit diff + governing-doc excerpts + expected output format) would make post-merge review cheap enough to always run.

## Scope
- ddx bead review <bead-id> [--iter N] [--output <file>]
- Pulls bead AC from the bead record
- Pulls commit diff via git show (latest successful execute-bead result_rev by default, or --from-rev)
- Reads governing-doc slugs from bead 'spec-id' / 'refs' fields and excerpts the relevant sections
- Renders a prompt template asking the agent to grade each AC item and produce structured APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK
- Output is the prompt body (stdout or to a file); pipe into ddx agent run --prompt @-

## Context
Observed during axon ADR-018 loop: I wrote /tmp/review-A1.md by hand twice (iter 1 and iter 2). Both prompts had the same structure. A templated command would eliminate that manual step and make reviews reproducible.","events":[{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on.    - workerLog(workerID: ID!): String! (covers GET /api/agent/workers/{id}/log)
   - workerPrompt(workerID: ID!): String! (covers GET /api/agent/workers/{id}/prompt)
   - agentCoordinators: [CoordinatorMetrics!]! (list form for GET /api/agent/coordinators)
   - documentByPath(path: String!): Document (covers GET /api/documents/{path...})

2. ADD per-field doc comments. Only type-level comments exist today. Acceptance criterion (3) says every field needs a doc comment. Add """...""" block above every field in every type. Biggest lift.

3. CONVERT search: [SearchResult!]! to SearchResultConnection with edges/node/cursor/pageInfo per the Relay cursor rule.

4. Leave existing content in cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql alone where possible — this is an edit pass, not a rewrite.

Opus fresh-eyes review against prior fix list:

FIXED (5 of 8):
- health: HealthStatus! added (line 791)
- ready: ReadyStatus! added (line 794)
- workerLog added (returns WorkerLog! not String! — acceptable, richer)
- workerPrompt added
- agentCoordinators added (renamed to coordinators — semantically equivalent)

NOT FIXED (3 of 8):
1. documentByPath(path: String!): Document — STILL MISSING. GET /api/documents/{path...} remains orphaned.
2. Per-field doc comments — STILL MOSTLY MISSING. New types added in this commit (CoordinatorMetrics, HealthStatus, ReadyCheck, ReadyStatus, WorkerLog, LandOutcomeSummary, etc.) got per-field # comments, but the original 30+ types (NodeInfo, Project, Bead, Dependency, Document, DocumentReview, DocumentExecDef, ExecutorSpec, ResultSpec, MetricResultSpec, Evaluation, Thresholds, DocGraph, StaleReason, Commit, Worker, CurrentAttemptInfo, PhaseTransition, LastAttemptInfo, WorkerExecutionResult, AgentSession, TokenUsage, Persona, ExecutionRun, ExecutionDefinition, Provenance, MetricObservation, Provider, PageInfo, SearchResult, all Edge/Connection types) are untouched. 10-field sample from those types: 0/10 have per-field comments. Additionally, gqlgen does not emit # comments as GraphQL descriptions — the spec requires """...""" blocks. Recommend converting all to triple-quote descriptions.
3. search: [SearchResult!]! — STILL a plain list. SearchResultConnection/SearchResultEdge do not exist in the file.

REGRESSION:
- coordinatorMetricsByProject(projectID: ID!): CoordinatorMetrics! was silently dropped. GET /api/coordinator/metrics/{projectRoot} loses direct coverage.

VERDICT: REJECT. Retry with stronger model.

NEXT RETRY SHOULD:
1. Add documentByPath query
2. Add per-field descriptions (""" blocks, not # comments) to EVERY field in EVERY type. Start from ORIGINAL types not the new ones. Biggest lift — the model needs to grind through ~35 types.
3. Replace "search: [SearchResult!]!" with SearchResultConnection and add SearchResultConnection/SearchResultEdge types next to other connection types.
4. Restore coordinatorMetricsByProject(projectID) query.
5. verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/ -run TestFanOut -count=1 -timeout 60s

This bead (ddx-fe63969f) duplicates work already committed under bead
ddx-89bc2db4 in commit ef7efd46 ("feat(federation): hub-side fan-out
client/merger with timeout, max-concurrency, partial results").

Acceptance-criteria mapping to existing code:

AC1 — Fan-out client with timeout + concurrency bounds.
  FanOutClient at cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:125-141 with
  MaxConcurrency (default 8) + PerNodeTimeout (default 5s). Concurrency
  is enforced by a buffered channel semaphore at fanout.go:226. Per-node
  timeout is applied via context.WithTimeout in callOne at fanout.go:329.

AC2 — Merger marks partials; never silently drops.
  FanOutResult at fanout.go:103-122 splits results into Responses,
  Errors, Skipped, StatusUpdates, and Nodes. Every input spoke produces
  exactly one NodeResult (fanout.go:225, 266-289). NodeOutcomeKind
  values OutcomeTimeout / OutcomeOffline / OutcomeHTTPError /
  OutcomeSkipped (fanout.go:46-65) distinguish partial-failure modes.
  Per-node timeouts deliberately do NOT flip status to offline
  (fanout.go:353-361), preserving the registry's stale signal.

AC3 — Per-spoke capability check skips incompatible spokes gracefully.
  preflightSkip at fanout.go:295-323 filters by RequiredCapabilities
  against SpokeRecord.Capabilities (ignoring the synthetic @identity:
  fingerprint cap), emitting OutcomeSkipped + SkipReasonMissingCapability.
  Version incompatibility is also handled (SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion
  via the Handshake gate).

AC4 — Tests cover timeout, partial, and capability skip.
  cli/internal/federation/fanout_test.go covers:
    - TestFanOut_PerNodeTimeoutDoesNotBlockOthers (timeout)
    - TestFanOut_PartialResultOnOneFailure (partial)
    - TestFanOut_StaleVsOfflineDistinct (partial / status-update policy)
    - TestFanOut_MidResponseDisconnect (partial / transport failure)
    - TestFanOut_HTTPErrorIsNotOffline (partial / classification)
    - TestFanOut_MissingCapabilitySkipped (capability skip)
    - TestFanOut_VersionIncompatibleSpokesSkipped (version skip)
    - TestFanOut_MaxConcurrencyEnforced (concurrency bound)
    - TestFanOut_AllSpokesSucceed, TestFanOut_PostsGraphQLPayload,
      TestFanOut_RejectsEmptyRequest (happy path / contract).

The verification command above re-runs the suite and exits 0 when all
AC-covering tests pass.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1157:{"acceptance":"(1) Root cause documented in code comment next to SynthesizeCommit. (2) SynthesizeCommit completes with exit 0 when only change is in a gitignored directory. (3) Regression test: a project with non-empty .ddx/agent-logs and no real changes yields SynthesizeCommit → (false, nil) (no commit, no error). (4) Regression test: a project with one real modification + non-empty .ddx/agent-logs yields (true, nil) with only the real change committed. (5) ddx work --once drains ddx-672cf2bf or another ready bead end-to-end without hitting staging-changes exit 1.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:57:38.058551192Z","description":"Blocks `ddx work` whenever the gitignored runtime directories `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` have any content. Every real-world project hits this after the first agent run.

## Symptom

Every tier attempt in the execute-loop aborts:

```
pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging changes: exit status 1
```

## Root cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:363 `RealGitOps.SynthesizeCommit` runs:

```
git -C <dir> add -A -- . ':(exclude).ddx/agent-logs' ':(exclude).ddx/workers' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt' ':(exclude).claude/skills' ':(exclude).agents/skills'
```

When `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` exist as untracked directories (normal after the first agent run), and both are in .gitignore (lines 421-422), git add emits:

```
The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files:
.ddx/agent-logs
.ddx/workers
hint: Use -f if you really want to add them.
```

AND exits with status 1. The `:(exclude)` magic pathspec is not sufficient to silence this exit-1 in git's add behavior with `-C` form — even `-c advice.addIgnoredFile=false` does not suppress it.

## Fix candidates (not pre-decided)

1. Check `git status --porcelain` first; only run `git add` if there are tracked changes to stage.
2. Enumerate dirty files in Go (via `git status --porcelain`), filter out excluded paths, pass the survivors explicitly to `git add --`.
3. Distinguish stderr "benign ignored-files warning" from real errors; accept exit 1 when that's the only message.
4. Move `.ddx/agent-logs/` and `.ddx/workers/` out of the repo tree so they're outside the pathspec entirely.

Prefer (1) or (2) — they're honest about the intent "stage only real modifications." Option (3) is fragile against git message changes; (4) touches a lot of downstream tooling.

## Evidence

Worker: worker-20260420T145025-61dc. Bead ddx-672cf2bf. All three tier attempts (cheap/standard/smart) failed identically. Confirmed reproducible via manual run of the exact add command in `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.

## Relationship to other beads

Independent of ddx-672cf2bf (silent-exit on epic queues) — this is loud-exit on staging. Independent of v0.7.0. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1162:{"acceptance":"1. Pan/zoom works on first render with ResizeObserver; resizing window re-layouts graph.
2. Clicking a graph node navigates to the document/artifact detail without a full page reload.
3. Pressing Back from a document opened via graph click returns to graph with the same zoom/pan position.
4. Filter chips filter visible nodes + edges; node colors reflect staleness (green/amber/red).
5. 'View document list' link on graph page navigates to /artifacts?mediaType=text%2Fmarkdown.
6. graph.spec.ts covers: initial render → pan/zoom → click node → verify navigation → Back → verify viewport restored → apply staleness filter → verify node count changes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-01T03:06:28Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3396374","closing_commit_sha":"7123e6ebd786c63225500b5544b95be2901b60d4","created_at":"2026-04-30T15:10:47.923008659Z","description":"Fix the document dependency graph interactivity. Addresses US-081 AC which is specced but unimplemented.

Bugs to fix:
1. Pan/zoom sizing race: D3Graph.svelte uses el.clientWidth at init time which may be 0. Replace with ResizeObserver: set up simulation once observer fires with non-zero dimensions. This also handles window resize.
2. Node click = no handler: nodeGroup has mouseenter/mousemove/mouseleave but no onclick. Add click handler that navigates to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents/:path (for markdown artifacts) or /artifacts/:artifactId (for other types). Distinguish by mediaType from graph node data.

Viewport persistence:
- Save zoom transform (x, y, k) to component state or URL param (e.g. ?zoom=1.2&pan=120,45) on each zoom/pan end event.
- On Back navigation, restore the saved transform before the D3 effect runs.

New features:
- Filter chips on graph page: filter nodes by mediaType (markdown, image, unknown) and staleness (fresh, stale, missing). Hidden nodes removed from simulation; edges to hidden nodes removed.
- Node color = staleness: green=fresh, amber=stale, red=missing (not dependsOn.length as today).
- 'View document list' link on graph page → /documents (which redirects to /artifacts).
- 'View in Graph' link on artifact detail page (once ddx-d7d1119d exists) → /graph with node pre-selected.

Review area: host+user ddx-server planning stack after the server model evolved beyond the earlier multi-project topology draft. Evidence covers FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, TP-002, the fresh-eyes server review, and the updated requirements for a per-user host daemon, localhost HTTP UI, SQLite-backed runtime/index state, embedded-agent progress visibility, provider dashboarding, replay-backed fixtures, and Playwright coverage for every new UI surface.
</context-digest>
Update the governing server planning docs so they describe the current intended host+user ddx-server model before follow-on server design and implementation beads continue.

## Goals
- Reconcile FEAT-002, FEAT-008, FEAT-013, FEAT-014, SD-019, and TP-002 around a single host+user ddx-server instance with explicit project registry, worker management boundaries, and queue semantics
- Define the split between git-backed project truth, tracked execution evidence, and host-local runtime/index state backed by an embedded database
- Define the current transport/security model: localhost unauthenticated HTTP for UI/API now, explicit future transport/auth evolution later
- Add the embedded-agent progress, provider dashboard, replay-backed fixture, and Playwright coverage requirements to the governed server plan
- Make the scheduling boundary explicit: server may allocate workers across projects, but bead ordering remains owned by each project queue

## Boundaries
- Keep Axon and multi-host service-backed topology as later-stage work
- Do not reopen the already-shipped single-project execute-bead loop contract except where the new server plan must reference it","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"checkpointing dirty worktree: git stash: error: could not write index

Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should run `ddx agent execute-bead` and then separately run required post-run checks, classify the outcome, and apply land/preserve semantics. FEAT-006 already defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the canonical workflow that resolves execution documents, runs required executions, evaluates merge eligibility, and either lands or preserves the iteration. Implementing API-001 as written would either duplicate required executions and landing logic around `execute-bead` or force FEAT-006 to become stale without saying so, which makes the command/supervisor boundary ambiguous for the first shipped worker.
Suggested fix: Make one layer authoritative. File: cli/internal/registry/audit.go:24
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `AuditInstalledEntry` unconditionally adds `missing package.yaml` whenever `loadPackageDefinitionForAudit` returns `manifestPresent=false`. For malformed manifests, `loadPackageDefinitionForAudit` also returns `false`, so doctor reports both the real validation problem and a bogus missing-file error. Repro: a plugin root with `api_version: [not-a-scalar]` produces both `unsupported api_version "[not-a-scalar]"` and `missing package.yaml`.
Suggested fix: Distinguish `manifest missing` from `manifest invalid` in the audit helper and only append `missing package.yaml` for the `os.IsNotExist` case.","id":"hx-83e2d5d9","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/internal/registry/audit.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: avoid reporting malformed manifests as missing package.yaml","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:24:08.584604731Z"}

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 chooses a candidate using the generic execution-ready validator before it resolves the base revision and governing execution-contract snapshot. FEAT-006 says execution documents are resolved from the base revision before the agent runs, and those pre-run versions govern the current iteration. If the supervisor validates selection against HEAD or server state first and only later resolves `--from` or checkpoint-derived base state, it can launch a bead against a different execution-definition snapshot than the one it validated.

Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should read the ready bead set and choose the best candidate using the generic execution-ready validator. But the same contract says HELIX owns which beads are eligible for the loop, and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering as priority then stable snapshot order. The generic DDx validator can say whether one bead is structurally executable; it does not define queue priority or workflow policy. Leaving candidate choice as 'best' via the validator either invents hidden DDx scheduling policy or makes the first worker's queue behavior undefined.
Suggested fix: Separate queue ordering from readiness validation. Specify whether the supervisor consumes a pre-ordered execution-ready queue (for example SD-004 ordering plus workflow eligibility filters) and then uses the validator only as a gate on the selected candidate.","id":"hx-ec50ff6b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:api","area:agent","area:bead","area:docs"],"parent":"ddx-315eddb0","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"drift: define supervisor queue selection independently from the generic validator","updated_at":"2026-04-10T16:18:32.447577504Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:1461:{"acceptance":"execute-bead rebases the worktree result onto current HEAD before attempting ff-merge; when the rebase succeeds the result lands without a merge commit; when the rebase conflicts the iteration is preserved under the hidden ref and the target branch remains unchanged; and tests cover the case where the target branch advanced during execution","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T02:28:56Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4071725","closing_commit_sha":"65134ca7caedd43b6dab6e567d001b36fb32b665","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","depends_on_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","type":"blocks"}],"description":"FEAT-006 step 10 specifies 'land by rebase + fast-forward semantics' but the implementation only attempts `git merge --ff-only`.

## Current behavior
If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. If the rebase fails because of conflicts, preserve the iteration under the hidden ref, leave the target branch untouched, and report the non-landed result deterministically.","id":"hx-exec-bead-rebase-ff","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Implement rebase + ff semantics for execute-bead land flow","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:23.760412906Z"}

## Current behavior
When execute-bead detects a dirty worktree, it calls git stash to checkpoint changes but never calls git stash pop. User's in-progress work remains silently stashed.

## Fix required
Add git stash pop (or git stash apply) in the defer cleanup after the worktree is removed, or document that stash is intentional and warn the user with the stash ref.","id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Fix execute-bead dirty worktree stash/restore bug","updated_at":"2026-04-09T19:20:21.950105538Z"}

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:67
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The updated contract still says the supervisor claims the bead, creates an isolated execution worktree, runs `ddx agent execute-bead` inside that worktree, and then removes the worktree. FEAT-006 defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the workflow that creates the isolated worktree (step 4) and always removes it after preserve/land handling (step 12), and the shipped command implementation already does exactly that in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:261-274`. As written, the first shipped supervisor cannot follow API-001 without layering a second worktree lifecycle around one execute-bead attempt or introducing an undocumented lower-level mode. That leaves the core ownership boundary ambiguous and makes the contract non-executable for the intended wrapper design.
Suggested fix: Make one layer own the worktree lifecycle. Either change API-001 so the supervisor invokes `ddx agent execute-bead` from the project root and only reacts to its result, or introduce and document a lower-level execute-bead primitive that accepts an already-created worktree and update FEAT-006/API-001 together.","id":"hx-f9928f75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high","area:api","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead worktree ownership in API-001","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:45:39.684926309Z"}

func executeWithStdoutCapture(t *testing.T, root *cobra.Command, args ...string) (string, error) {
	t.Helper()

	stdoutR, stdoutW, err := os.Pipe()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	stderrR, stderrW, err := os.Pipe()
	require.NoError(t, err)

	origStdout := os.Stdout
	origStderr := os.Stderr
	os.Stdout = stdoutW
	os.Stderr = stderrW
	defer func() {
		os.Stdout = origStdout
		os.Stderr = origStderr
	}()

	var outBuf bytes.Buffer
	var errBuf bytes.Buffer
	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	wg.Add(2)
	go func() {
		defer wg.Done()
		_, _ = io.Copy(&outBuf, stdoutR)
	}()
	go func() {
		defer wg.Done()
		_, _ = io.Copy(&errBuf, stderrR)
	}()

	root.SetArgs(args)
	err = root.Execute()

	_ = stdoutW.Close()
	_ = stderrW.Close()
	wg.Wait()

	return outBuf.String() + errBuf.String(), err
}

package cmd

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")

	stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "WARNING: installed ddx is built from")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "commits ahead")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "Run \"make install\" to refresh.")
}

func TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	headSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")

	_, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", headSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, stderr)
}

func TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/example/not-ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")

	_, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, stderr)
}

func TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	headSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "detached\n", "detached")

	detach := exec.Command("git", "checkout", "--detach", headSHA)
	detach.Dir = repoDir
	detach.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	out, err := detach.CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git checkout --detach: %s", string(out))

	stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
	assert.NotEmpty(t, stderr)
}

func initVersionTestRepo(t *testing.T, originURL string) string {
	t.Helper()

	repoDir := t.TempDir()
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "init")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "config", "user.name", "Test User")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "remote", "add", "origin", originURL)
	return repoDir
}

func gitCommitFile(t *testing.T, repoDir, relPath, content, commitMsg string) string {
	t.Helper()

	fullPath := filepath.Join(repoDir, relPath)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(fullPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0o644))
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "add", relPath)
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "commit", "-m", commitMsg)
	return runVersionGitOutput(t, repoDir, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
}

func runVersionCommand(t *testing.T, workDir, version, commit string) (string, string, error) {
	t.Helper()

	factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	factory.Version = version
	factory.Commit = commit
	factory.Date = "2026-05-05T00:00:00Z"

	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	root.SetArgs([]string{"version"})

	var stdoutBuf bytes.Buffer
	var stderrBuf bytes.Buffer
	root.SetOut(&stdoutBuf)
	root.SetErr(&stderrBuf)

	err := root.Execute()
	return stdoutBuf.String(), stderrBuf.String(), err
}

func runVersionGit(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
	cmd.Dir = dir
	cmd.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
}

func runVersionGitOutput(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
	cmd.Dir = dir
	cmd.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	out, err := cmd.Output()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git %v", args)
	return string(bytes.TrimSpace(out))
}

data.text: `go test ./cmd/...` is failing for an environmental reason, not from this change: the installation acceptance tests expect `cli/build/ddx` to exist and it isn’t built in this worktree. I’m building the CLI binary now and then rerunning the command suite so the required gate reflects the actual code state.

   550	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
   551		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
   552			return
   553		}
   554		if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
   555			return
   556		}
   557	
   558		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
   559		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
   560			return
   561		}
   562	
   563		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
   564		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
   565			return
   566		}
   567	
   568		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, f.Commit)
   569		if err != nil {
   570			return
   571		}
   572		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
   573		if err != nil {
   574			return
   575		}
   576	
   577		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
   578			return
   579		}
   580	
   581		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
   582		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
   583			return
   584		}
   585	
   586		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
   587			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
   588			buildSHA, headSHA, aheadCount)
   589	}
   590	
   591	func gitCommandOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
   592		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
   593		if err != nil {
   594			return "", err
   595		}
   596		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
   597	}
   598	
   599	func gitResolveCommit(dir, commit string) (string, error) {
   600		return gitCommandOutput(dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commit+"^{commit}")
   601	}
   602	
   603	func isDDXOriginURL(url string) bool {
   604		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(url), "documentdrivendx/ddx")
   605	}

   620		if cfg.Agent != nil && cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir != "" {

Implemented and committed as `8c9432b7` (`fix(cli): warn when ddx version is stale [ddx-633b16eb]`).

What changed:
- [`cli/cmd/command_factory.go:457-604`] `ddx version` now checks whether the current repo’s `origin` points at `DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`, resolves the binary’s build SHA, compares it to `HEAD`, and prints the stale-binary warning to stderr when the source tree is ahead.
- [`cli/cmd/version_staleness_test.go:15-137`] Added the requested coverage:
  - `TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr`
  - `TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync`
  - `TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo`
  - `TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead`

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -count=1`
- `cd cli && CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race ./cmd/... -count=1`
- `cd cli && CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

The warning exits 0 and only emits when the local source tree is ahead of the installed binary.
